title: United States and Iran escalate conflict with mutual attacks on military facilities
summary: U.S. forces repeatedly strike Iranian targets in response to alleged ceasefire violations, while Trump warns of severe consequences.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela leaves over 1,400 dead and millions in economic losses
summary: The magnitude 6+ earthquake shakes the north of the country, leaving numerous casualties and significant material damage, while rescue efforts are complicated by hundreds of aftershocks.

title: Legendary Japanese artist Miwa Akihiro dies at 91
summary: Known for his music and theater, Miwa Akihiro dies of old age, leaving an indelible legacy in Japanese popular culture.

title: Strong magnitude 6.1 earthquake shakes Aomori and Iwate prefectures without triggering tsunami
summary: The earthquake, with epicenter off the coast of Iwate, reaches a seismic intensity of 5 in some areas, causing building damage but no tsunami warning.

title: England defeats Panama and secures top spot in Group L at the World Cup
summary: With goals from Jude Bellingham and Harry Kane, England wins 2-0 and advances first to the round of 16, while Croatia finishes second.

title: Japan prepares for crucial clash against Brazil at the World Cup
summary: The Japanese national team completes its preparation for the match against Brazil, while the media analyzes the strengths of the Seleção and the assigned referee.

title: Democratic Republic of Congo achieves first World Cup victory and eliminates South Korea
summary: In Group K, the DRC beats Uzbekistan and advances to the round of 16, while South Korea's defeat leaves them out of the tournament.

title: Argentina takes the lead, Spain and Belgium secure qualification and other World Cup results
summary: With several matches played, Argentina takes the lead, Spain, Belgium and Cape Verde confirm their qualification to the round of 16, and Iran adds a point in their group.

title: The difficulties of balancing work and family in Japan: surveys reveal shortages in daycare centers
summary: Studies show that many families avoid parental leave due to lack of spaces in childcare centers, while municipalities expand free school lunch programs.

title: Health and aging in Japan: from innovative caregivers to research against cognitive decline
summary: Initiatives such as singing caregivers and lifestyle intervention programs aim to improve the quality of life of the elderly, while home-delivered meal services face challenges.

title: Egypt reaches the World Cup round of 16 for the first time
summary: The Egyptian national team celebrates its historic qualification to the knockout stage, with fans unleashing euphoria and some excesses.

title: Hezbollah rejects border security agreement with Israel
summary: The leader of the Lebanese group calls the pact that cedes sovereignty null and void, while Israel insists on maintaining a security zone on the border.

title: Japanese electric power company illegally recorded court hearings
summary: Company employees admit to having recorded proceedings without permission, focused on lawsuits related to nuclear energy.

title: Europe suffers historic heatwave with record temperatures in Germany and France
summary: Germany records 41.5°C and Paris reports more than a hundred deaths due to extreme heat.

title: Chinese and Russian fighter jets conduct joint maneuvers near Japanese airspace
summary: Four bombers fly in formation over the Sea of Japan and the East China Sea, without violating sovereignty, in a show of military pressure.

title: One-vote victory in Nasu mayoral election overturned by court
summary: The electoral court invalidates the result of the municipal election, and the elected mayor appeals to the Tokyo court.

title: Hinako Shibuno rises to second place at the PGA Women's Open
summary: The Japanese golfer improves her position in the third round of the tournament, staying five strokes behind the leader.

title: Russia intensifies pressure on Belarus to directly participate in the war in Ukraine
summary: An estimated 50,000 Russian soldiers could be ready, while the UN warns of the enormous environmental impact of the conflict.

title: Minister Takaichi visits India and Japanese Defense Minister tours South Korea
summary: In a week of intense diplomatic activity, Japan strengthens security ties with both countries and studies the use of unmanned submarines in the Pacific.

title: Analysts debate the social and tactical impact of the World Cup
summary: Opinion columns address blind football, changes in game strategies, and the meaning of victories in the tournament.
